LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT ROBIN KAY'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS DENIED. NEITHER RULE 9.23 NOR CONSTITUTIONAL DUE PROCESS REQUIRE THAT THERE BE NOTICE TO A DEFENDANT PRIOR TO THE COURT'S EXERCISE OF ITS MANDATORY DUTY TO ENTER JUDGMENT UPON THE REQUEST OF THE STATE BAR WHEN THE STATE BAR SUBMITS THE REQUIRED PAPERWORK. THE STATE BAR'S FAILURE TO SERVE MS. KAY OR MR. KAY'S COUNSEL WITH THE JUDGMENT AFTER IT WAS ENTERED DOES NOT RENDER THE JUDGMENT VOID OR OTHERWISE REQUIRE OR EVEN PERMIT THAT THE JUDGMENT BE SET ASIDE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

calendar.matters:
APPEAL OF CIVIL JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Affirmed
MINI MINUTES: appellant attorney, simone mccormick, present. respondent attorney, danielle lee, present.

APPEAL OF CIVIL JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2014 Continued To Mar-14-14 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 622

Defendant Robin Kay'S Motion For Set Aside/Vacate Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DANIELLE LEE, ASSISTANT GENERAL COUNSEL, 415.538.2517, FOR PLAINTIFF STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA.
THOMAS J. D'AMATO, ESQ., 415.788.1900, FOR DEFENDANT ROBIN KAY.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant Robin Kay's Motion For Set Aside/Vacate Judgment is denied. Neither Rule 9.23 nor constitutional due process require that there be notice to a defendant prior to the court's exercise of its mandatory duty to enter judgment upon the request of the State Bar when the State bar submits the required paperwork. The State Bar's failure to serve Ms. Kay or Mr. Kay's counsel with the judgment after it was entered does not render the judgment void or otherwise require or even permit that the judgment be set aside. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ROBIN KAY'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Continued to April 16, 2013 per the agreement of the parties. The hearing will be at 9 am, not 9:30 am. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: E. Larnauti, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185 (302/HEK)

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2013 Continued To Apr-15-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ROBIN KAY's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is continued to be heard on April 15, 2013 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 302 before Judge Kahn. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
